diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers keep the same unsupported label but justify it differently. On citations, option 1 relies only on evidence-002 (outdated info), while option 2 cites both evidence-001 (secondhand competitor info) and evidence-002. Because the claim concerns pricing against secondhand competitor data, citing both the secondhand nature and the outdated nature is more diagnostically adequate than citing only outdatedness, so the citation difference is not merely redundant subset use. On reasons, option 1 frames the problem as contradiction/counterexample preserved, whereas option 2 frames it as lack of support for the central proposition. Given the evidence says the competitor data is secondhand and outdated and that current pricing needs current data, these reason codes capture different aspects of why the claim is unsupported, making them complementary rather than one clearly better. Confidence is not meaningfully different because both are high.
